mission.statement: At the SNF Agora Institute's Center for Economy and Society, we reinvigorate core debates about political economy. As scholars, teachers, and community-builders, our ideas move freely across disciplinary and ideological boundaries. Historically, fixed models of political economy have obscured potential avenues for innovation. Here, through generative disagreement and productive discourse, we explore those hidden opportunities and develop actionable solutions to address pressing global challenges.
mission.funding_line: The Center for Economy and Society is one of a number of inter-related initiatives funded by the Hewlett Foundation and Omidyar Network.

pillars.name: Discovery
pillars.subtitle: Uncovering Points of Convergence
pillars.description: By reframing conversations and reorienting debates along different axes, we aim to reopen static debates in political economy, make disagreement more fruitful, and discover unexpected points of convergence.

pillars.name: Education
pillars.subtitle: Cultivating Leaders for Tomorrow
pillars.description: Our goal is to cultivate a new generation of students who are poised to tackle the complex political and economic issues of our era, through deep reading, rigorous research methods, and communal engagement.

pillars.name: Connection
pillars.subtitle: Building Bridges, Shaping Futures
pillars.description: As a professional network and research center, we serve as an incubator of ideas and a hub for exchanging ideas across sectors and channels.

focus_areas.title: AI and the Economy
focus_areas.description: Delving into how artificial intelligence is redefining economic landscapes.

focus_areas.title: Economic Security
focus_areas.description: Examining the foundations of financial stability in modern society.

focus_areas.title: The New Abundance Economics Agenda
focus_areas.description: Charting a new course for prosperity in today's world.

focus_areas.title: Innovations in the Infrastructures of Care
focus_areas.description: Reimagining support systems for the 21st century.

focus_areas.title: U.S.-China and the World
focus_areas.description: Navigating the complexities of global economic leadership.

focus_areas.title: Poverty and Development
focus_areas.description: Tackling the root causes of poverty with fresh insights.

focus_areas.title: The Economics of the Workplace
focus_areas.description: Understanding the evolving dynamics of our workplaces.

focus_areas.title: Political Economies of Exclusion
focus_areas.description: Addressing the barriers that divide us.

programs.name: BA in Moral and Political Economy
programs.description: A new undergraduate major at Johns Hopkins that teaches students to think about economic problems in their moral, social, and political contexts. It is Johns Hopkins' only selective major in the humanities and social sciences, and requires all students to complete senior honors theses. Contact: mpe.dus@jh.edu.

programs.name: enCOREage
programs.description: A collaboration among CORE Econ, the Center for Advanced Study in the Behavioral Sciences (CASBS) at Stanford, and Honor Education, creating a free, open-access introductory economics e-textbook, "Understanding the Economy," addressing inequality, innovation, climate change, and the future of work.

activities.name: District Dinners
activities.description: Monthly salon-style dinner conversations in DC bringing together journalists, researchers, think-tankers, and policymakers across three thematic areas: economic statecraft; American political economy; and US-China in a global perspective.

activities.name: Scholarship and Journalism
activities.description: Alongside commissioning traditional academic papers for peer-reviewed journals, CES prioritizes sharing knowledge with major media outlets such as The Economist, The New York Times, The Wall Street Journal, and C-SPAN.

activities.name: Visiting Fellowships
activities.description: Each year, CES extends visiting fellowships to several DC-area journalists whose investigative interests align with its mission, strengthening ties between research and reporting.

opportunities.name: Postdoctoral Fellowships in Moral and Political Economy
opportunities.description: Two or more three-year fellowships. Fellows teach two sections/seminars in year one and three in years two and three, advise senior thesis students, and remain in residence in the Baltimore-Washington region.

opportunities.name: Research Assistantships
opportunities.description: Paid academic-year opportunities matching Moral & Political Economy majors with CES faculty and fellows.

opportunities.name: Undergraduate Summer Internship Program
opportunities.description: Full-time summer internships placing students with leading international development organizations.

funding_sources: William and Flora Hewlett Foundation ($10 million)
Stavros Niarchos Foundation (via SNF Agora Institute endowment)
connected_networks: SNF Agora Institute at Johns Hopkins University
Johns Hopkins Data Science and AI Institute
JHU Krieger School of Arts and Sciences
about.parent_institute: Housed within the SNF Agora Institute at Johns Hopkins University, 3100 Wyman Park Dr, Baltimore, MD 21218.

related_resources.title: BA in Moral and Political Economy
related_resources.description: Program prepares students to tackle challenges of a complex world

related_resources.title: Numbers and Nerds: A Crash Course in Capitalism
related_resources.description: Related resource linked from the current CES page — verify what this is (podcast/book/course) during research.
